perturbation.original_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure as being confidential and proprietary. The thirty (30) day period for designation shall not apply.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the thirty-day grace period for designating information as confidential. This creates a contradiction because other sections of the agreement assume that there is a 30-day window to designate, contradicting the removal of the time frame.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for a 30-day window to designate information as confidential. The changed text eliminates this 30-day window, requiring immediate designation. The contradicted text implicitly assumes the existence of confidential information disclosures. This creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted text refers to actions concerning "each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION". If the designation has to happen immediately, it makes it contradictory with the need to take further actions to protect the confidentiality of that information. Based on the Perturbation Explanation, the thirty-day grace period for designating information as confidential creates a contradiction because other sections of the agreement assume that there is a 30-day window to designate, contradicting the removal of the time frame.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Any Party, at its discretion, may disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Any Party *must* disclose to another Party any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that the disclosing Party, in its reasonable judgment, believes is sufficient to enable the receiving Party to arrive at conceptions, ideas, innovations, discoveries, inventions, compositions, biological material, biological methods, whether or not patentable or susceptible to any other form of legal protection, during performance Under the IRA.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'may disclose' to '*must* disclose' changes the permissive nature of disclosure to a mandatory one. This contradicts the implicit understanding that parties can choose what information to share, creating an obligation. The contradiction arises with the freedom and right which is stated on Paragraph 1, of only disclosing confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary. No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction. Specific aspects or details of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'may disclose' to 'must disclose' creates a direct contradiction. The original text provided discretion in disclosing confidential information, while the changed text mandates disclosure. This contradicts the contradicted text, Paragraph 1, which implies a right to withhold confidential information by stating that information is disclosed 'pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary,' suggesting that the party can choose whether or not to identify it as confidential in the first place. Making disclosure mandatory removes this implicit right, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for no other purpose; (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. In consideration of each and every disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the Parties agree to: (a) treat as confidential and to preserve the confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION; (b) use any and all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION solely in connection with the performance of the IRA and for *any* purpose; (c) make no disclosures of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any party other than officers and employees of a Party to this IRA; (d) limit access to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to those officers and employees having a reasonable need for such INFORMATION and being boUnd by a written obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such INFORMATION; and (e) maintain in confidence any information regarding the nature or scope of any transaction between the Parties, except to the extent such information must be disclosed pursuant to law, and then only after notifying the other Party of such requirement.
perturbation.explanation: The change in section 3(b) from 'solely in connection with the performance of the IRA' to 'for *any* purpose' broadens the scope of permitted use of confidential information. This contradicts the fundamental purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, which is to restrict the use of confidential information to a specific purpose and therefore, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary. No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction. Specific aspects or details of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the permitted use of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' from 'solely in connection with the performance of the IRA' to 'for *any* purpose'. This directly contradicts the definition of 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' which specifies that it is disclosed 'pursuant to the JRA' implying a restricted use. By allowing use for 'any purpose', the changed text nullifies the purpose of maintaining confidentiality and creates a strong in-text contradiction with the defined scope and intended restrictions of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION elsewhere in the document.

perturbation.original_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party. *The receiving Party may retain a copy of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, even after the request of the disclosing party.* Upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'The receiving Party may retain a copy of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, even after the request of the disclosing party' after stating that all confidential information will remain the property of the disclosing party. This contradicts the very concept of ownership and the right to recall property, and weakens the confidentiality protection by allowing the receiving party to retain copies even after the disclosing party asks for their return. The obligation for the receiving party to 'promptly return' the information is undermined by the new provision, creating an enforceable and textual contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party. *The receiving Party may retain a copy of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, even after the request of the disclosing party.* Upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that upon request, the receiving party 'shall promptly return' all confidential information. The changed text adds a provision that allows the receiving party to 'retain a copy' even after the disclosing party requests its return. This directly conflicts with the obligation to return all confidential information and undermines the concept of the disclosing party's property rights, as highlighted in the explanation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the change creates an in-text contradiction that did not exist before. Thus, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction as it breaks the consistency of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA. *However, obligations related to trade secrets will continue indefinitely.*
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the expiry condition from a fixed term of 5 years, to infinite obligations on trade secrets. The new condition contradicts the initially stated expiry and introduces ambiguity around which obligations are still valid once the 5 year period expires. The term trade secret is not previously defined in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA. *However, obligations related to trade secrets will continue indefinitely.*
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all obligations expire after five years from the JRA termination. The changed text introduces an exception for obligations related to trade secrets, which are now indefinite. This creates a direct conflict with the original expiry term, resulting in an in-text contradiction. The changed text makes unclear which obligations cease after 5 years, and which continue indefinitely, therefore it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. *However, implicit rights to use confidential information for internal research and development purposes are granted.*
perturbation.explanation: This explicitly states that 'no right or license' is granted, yet adds a contradictory clause allowing 'implicit rights to use confidential information for internal research and development purposes'. This introduces a direct contradiction by granting limited rights while also denying any rights are granted, leading to uncertainty about the extent to which parties can use confidential information. The use of confidential information contradicts the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. *However, implicit rights to use confidential information for internal research and development purposes are granted.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating 'no right or license' is granted, but then immediately adds an exception allowing 'implicit rights to use confidential information for internal research and development purposes.' This directly contradicts the initial statement and the general purpose of an NDA, creating ambiguity about the allowable use of confidential information, as the contradicted text is the changed text. This adheres to the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
